reasoning: TSM is the best semiconductor business in the world and the bull case is air-tight. But after an 87% gain, with the stock at 12.9% of portfolio (largest position), and geopolitical risk elevated, this is the right moment to trim methodically. Sell enough to bring TSM to ~8% of portfolio (approximately 10 of the 25 shares). This locks in substantial gains, reduces single-stock concentration risk, and you still keep meaningful exposure to the AI infrastructure theme. The risk/reward at current levels, given geopolitical uncertainty and the size of this position, does not justify holding at 13%. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Reduce ~10 shares now (bring to ~8% of portfolio, ~₪15,500 ILS). Hold remainder with stop-loss at $290 (200d MA at ~₪9,150 ILS). POSITION SIZE: ~₪25,738 (~12

bullCase: TSMC is the irreplaceable infrastructure of the AI era. 20.5% revenue growth, 62.3% margins, guided ~30% for 2026. Forward P/E 20.3x vs sector 21.7x — cheap for what it is. $240B+ backlog, net cash $62.7B. VP insider buying in March. Position up 87% — the market has been right to own this.
bearCase: Taiwan geopolitical risk is real and underappreciated. Iran conflict threatens Strait of Hormuz LNG routes — Taiwan's energy supply depends on it. Revenue decelerating from 41.6% to 20.5% YoY. MACD bearish, price below 50d MA. At 12.9% of portfolio, this is the largest position by weight AND the most geopolitically exposed. A 20% drawdown on TSM alone would cost you 2.6% of your entire portfolio in one move.
